PROMPT: I'm researching national parks in the western United States for a travel planning project. Please do the following: Search for all national parks in California (state code 'CA'), then from that list identify the park whose full description mentions 'sequoia' or 'giant trees' — note its park code. Next, get the full details of that park and record its GPS coordinates (latitude and longitude). Then search for national parks in Utah (state code 'UT') and find the park with the word 'canyon' in its name that has the most visitor amenities mentioned — note its park code. Get the full details of that Utah park and record its GPS coordinates. Now use those two sets of GPS coordinates to calculate the driving distance and travel time between those two parks using Google Maps. Additionally, get the current weather at the California park's coordinates and the 7-day forecast at the Utah park's coordinates. Finally, check for any active alerts at both parks. Summarize: park names, their coordinates, the driving distance and estimated time between them, current temperature at the California park, the highest forecasted high temperature in the next 7 days at the Utah park, and whether either park has active alerts.

"required_tool": "national-parks_findParks", "output_characteristics": "Up to 50 park objects each with ~20+ fields including fullName, parkCode, description, activities, topics, addresses, images, entranceFees, operatingHours"}, {"step": 2, "description": "Extract Signal A: scan all CA park descriptions and names for 'sequoia' or 'giant trees', record the matching parkCode", "required_tool": null, "extraction_criteria": "Find park whose description or fullName contains 'sequoia' or 'giant tree' (case-insensitive)", "signal_to_remember": "CA_parkCode (e.g., 'sequ' for Sequoia NP)"}, {"step": 3, "description": "Call national-parks_getParkDetails using CA_parkCode from step 2, extract GPS coordinates (latitude, longitude) from latLong field", "required_tool": "national-parks_getParkDetails", "dependency_on_step": 2, "output_characteristics": "Single verbose park object with latLong string like 'lat:36.4, long:-118.5885335', plus dozens of other fields (contacts, multimedia, topics, etc.)", "signal_to_remember": "CA_lat, CA_lon"}, {"step": 4, "description": "Call national-parks_findParks for Utah (stateCode=UT, limit=50), returns large list of parks — many irrelevant", "required_tool": "national-parks_findParks", "output_characteristics": "Up to 50 Utah park objects with same verbose structure as step 1"}, {"step": 5, "description": "Extract Signal B: find UT park with 'canyon' in its fullName, record the parkCode", "required_tool": null, "extraction_criteria": "Find first park in UT results whose fullName contains 'canyon' (case-insensitive)", "signal_to_remember": "UT_parkCode (e.g., 'brca' for Bryce Canyon or 'zion' for Zion with canyon in name)"}, {"step": 6, "description": "Call national-parks_getParkDetails using UT_parkCode from step 5, extract GPS coordinates from latLong field", "required_tool": "national-parks_getParkDetails", "dependency_on_step": 5, "output_characteristics": "Verbose park object; agent must parse latLong string to extract numeric lat/lon", "signal_to_remember": "UT_lat, UT_lon"}, {"step": 7, "description": "Call google-maps_maps_distance_matrix using CA_lat/CA_lon (from step 3) as origin and UT_lat/UT_lon (from step 6) as destination with mode=driving", "required_tool": "google-maps_maps_distance_matrix", "dependency_on_step": [3, 6], "uses_signal": "CA_lat, CA_lon, UT_lat, UT_lon", "output_characteristics": "Nested JSON with rows>elements>distance.text and duration.text buried among status fields"}, {"step": 8, "description": "Call weather_get_current_weather using CA_lat,CA_lon coordinates (from step 3) to get current temperature at the California park", "required_tool": "weather_get_current_weather", "dependency_on_step": 3, "uses_signal": "CA_lat, CA_lon", "output_characteristics": "Large nested NWS-format JSON with properties.temperature.value buried among dozens of fields"}, {"step": 9, "description": "Call weather_get_weather_forecast with UT_lat,UT_lon coordinates (from step 6) and days=7, scan all forecast periods to find highest temperature value", "required_tool": "weather_get_weather_forecast", "dependency_on_step": 6, "uses_signal": "UT_lat, UT_lon", "output_characteristics": "JSON with properties.periods array of 14 day/night periods each with temperature, shortForecast, detailedForecast, windSpeed, etc."}, {"step": 10, "description": "Call national-parks_getAlerts for CA park using CA_parkCode (from step 2), check if any active alerts exist", "required_tool": "national-parks_getAlerts", "dependency_on_step": 2, "uses_signal": "CA_parkCode", "output_characteristics": "Array of alert objects with title, description, category, url — may be empty or contain many items"}, {"step": 11, "description": "Call national-parks_getAlerts for UT park using UT_parkCode (from step 5), check if any active alerts exist, then compile final summary", "required_tool": "national-parks_getAlerts", "dependency_on_step": 5, "uses_signal": "UT_parkCode", "output_characteristics": "Array of alert objects; final answer requires aggregating: park names, coordinates, driving distance, driving time, current CA temp, highest UT 7-day temp, alert status for both parks"}]
EVALUATION_RULES: {"required_tools": ["national-parks_findParks", "national-parks_getParkDetails", "national-parks_getAlerts", "google-maps_maps_distance_matrix", "weather_get_current_weather", "weather_get_weather_forecast"], "forbidden_tools": [], "minimum_tool_calls": 9, "signal_extraction_step": 2, "signal_usage_step": 7, "noise_characteristics": "Each findParks call returns up to 50 parks with full descriptions, contact info, hours, topics, activities, entrance fees, images, and addresses. getParkDetails returns verbose JSON with dozens of fields. getAlerts returns up to 50 alert objects each with title, description, category, url, lastIndexedDate. Distance matrix and weather forecast return multi-field nested objects. Agent must extract and retain two specific parkCodes and their GPS coordinates across 11 steps."}

I now have all the information needed. Let me compile the comprehensive summary for you. --- ## 🏞️ National Parks Research Summary ### Parks Identified | Park | State | Park Code | Coordinates | |------|-------|-----------|-------------| | **Sequoia & Kings Canyon National Parks** | California | `seki` | 36.7128°N, -118.5874°W | | **Bryce Canyon National Park** | Utah | `brca` | 37.5840°N, -112.1827°W | **Selection Criteria:** - **California Park**: Sequoia & Kings Canyon was selected because its description mentions "*the world's largest trees*" and "*ancient giant sequoias*" - directly referencing sequoias and giant trees. - **Utah Park**: Bryce Canyon was selected as the park with "canyon" in its name that has the most visitor amenities (29 activities including dining, RV camping, guided tours, museum exhibits, cross-country skiing, snowshoeing, biking, stargazing, and more). --- ### 🚗 Driving Distance & Travel Time | Route | Distance | Estimated Driving Time | |-------|----------|------------------------| | Sequoia & Kings Canyon → Bryce Canyon | **632 miles** | **9 hours 41 minutes** | *Via I-15 N through Bakersfield, Las Vegas, and into Utah* --- ### 🌡️ Weather Conditions | Location | Metric | Value | |----------|--------|-------| | **Sequoia & Kings Canyon (CA)** - Current | Temperature | **73°F** (22.8°C) | | | Humidity | 24% | | | Wind | 14 mph from the west | | **Bryce Canyon (UT)** - 7-Day Forecast | Highest Forecasted High | **77°F** (Saturday) | | | Weather Trend | Variable early week (55-66°F with rain/snow chances), warming to 70s later in the week with sunny skies | --- ### ⚠️ Active Alerts | Park | Alert Status | Details | |------|--------------|---------| | **Sequoia & Kings Canyon** | **YES - 2 Alerts** | 1️⃣ Winter Seasonal Road Closures: Mineral King Rd, Crescent Meadow/Moro Rock Roads, and Panoramic Point Road are closed due to snow conditions.
2️⃣ Storm Damage Closures: Middle Fork Rd, Redwood Canyon Rd, South Fork Rd, and Crystal Cave Rd are closed for repairs. | | **Bryce Canyon** | **YES - 1 Alert** | Wall Street side of Navajo Loop Trail is closed due to hazardous conditions. | --- ### 📋 Quick Reference Summary | Item | Value | |------|-------| | California Park | **Sequoia & Kings Canyon National Parks** | | California Park Coordinates | **36.7128°N, -118.5874°W** | | Utah Park | **Bryce Canyon National Park** | | Utah Park Coordinates | **37.5840°N, -112.1827°W** | | Driving Distance | **632 miles** | | Driving Time | **~9 hours 41 minutes** | | Current Temperature (CA) | **73°F** | | Highest 7-Day Forecast High (UT) | **77°F** | | Active Alerts at CA Park | **Yes** (2 road closures) | | Active Alerts at UT Park | **Yes** (1 trail closure) | --- **Planning Tips:** Both parks currently have partial closures, so be sure to check their websites before your trip. The drive between them is a full day's journey (~10 hours), so consider breaking it up with an overnight stop in Las Vegas or St. George, Utah.
